senses.definition: Present participle of shine; to make something shine or to be shining.
examples: She was shinning the silverware until it gleamed.
The sun was shinning through the window.

senses.definition: A type of informal climbing game or activity using a rope or stick; also a pipe or rod used for pulling or swinging.
examples: Children played shinny on the school field.
He used a shinny to reach the branch.

senses.definition: Present participle of shinny; to climb by using hands and knees, often up a pole or rope.
examples: The cat was shinnying up the tree.
They spent the afternoon shinnying along the fence.

senses.definition: A small, often irregularly shaped piece of paper used as a substitute for money or a local emergency coin; historically a small payment or allowance.
examples: The old coin was replaced by shinplasters during the shortage.
Collectibles include vintage shinplasters from the war era.

senses.definition: Plural of shinplaster; small pieces of paper money substitutes used in times of coin shortage.
examples: The vending machines accepted shinplasters during the crisis.
Stamp collecting of shinplasters became a hobby.

senses.definition: The front part of the leg between the knee and the ankle; the shin bones or the skin on that area.
examples: He bumped his shin on the chair.
She wore shin guards for the game.

senses.definition: Pain and inflammation along the inner part of the shinbone, usually due to overuse in runners.
examples: He developed shin splints after increasing his mileage too quickly.
Rest and ice help with shin splints.

senses.definition: In Japanese contexts, 'shintai' refers to the physical body or a sacred body; in some blends it can refer to a supposed material form of a deity.
examples: In Shinto, the deity's shintai is housed in the shrine.
Pilgrims honor the shintai during ceremonies.

senses.definition: Past tense of shintai in religious context; to treat as a sacred physical object or to honor a shintai.
examples: They shintied the sacred relic before the ceremony.
The artifact was shintied according to tradition.

senses.definition: Plural of shintie; diminutive or affectionate term for a young woman, primarily in some dialects or slang; may also refer to Shinto-related terms in certain contexts.
examples: The travelers greeted the shinties with smiles.
Some shinties were observed at the festival.

senses.definition: Demonym or identifier relating to Shintiyans; may refer to people or followers connected to Shinto ceremonial contexts.
examples: A Shintiyan artist presented a work inspired by shrine architecture.

senses.definition: Present participle of shinty; to play the traditional Scottish sport shinty, a team game played with sticks and ball.
examples: They were shinnying on the field, practicing shots with the sticks.
The team has been shinty-ing every weekend.

senses.definition: A mountain or hillside region name in certain locales; may refer to a local tribe or geography.
examples: The expedition passed through Shinwari territory.

senses.definition: A place name or fictional/less common term referring to a forested area; not a standard common noun.
examples: They hiked toward Shinwood to camp for the night.

senses.definition: Reflecting light; having a bright surface or luster.
examples: The shiny coin caught the sun.
She wore a shiny new dress.

senses.definition: A proper noun or tribal place name; not a common English term; may refer to a specific location or group.
examples: The delegation visited Shinza before the meeting.

senses.definition: A large watercraft. A vessel used for traveling on sea or oceans.
examples: The ship sailed across the Atlantic.
A cargo ship arrived this morning.

senses.definition: To send or transport something by ship; to travel by sea.
examples: They will ship the goods next week.
We ship worldwide.
